page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.s2.xlarge is a general-purpose virtual machine instance belonging to the ecs.s2 server family. Built on the KVM hypervisor and utilizing the x86_64 CPU architecture, this instance provides 2 dedicated vCPUs mapped directly to 2 physical cores, with 1.0 thread per core. This dedicated CPU allocation ensures that compute resources are not shared with other tenants, offering predictable processing capabilities for workloads.

The instance is equipped with 8.0 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced memory-to-core ratio of 4.0 GB per physical core. It does not include any local storage, meaning all storage requirements must be met via network-attached block storage. Additionally, the instance does not bundle any complimentary public IPv4 addresses or GPU hardware accelerators. Advanced CPU instruction sets, such as AVX, AVX-512, AMX, and hardware-accelerated cryptography, are not available on this hardware profile.

Given its dedicated dual-core configuration and balanced resource allocation, the ecs.s2.xlarge offers an entry-level dedicated hardware profile. This makes it a cost-efficient option for workloads requiring stable CPU performance without the overhead of larger instances. It is suitable for general-purpose applications, lightweight web serving, small databases, and basic development or testing environments that do not rely on specialized vector or matrix instruction sets.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.s2.xlarge is a general-purpose virtual machine instance from the ecs.s2 family, running on the KVM hypervisor with an x86_64 architecture. It features 2 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 2 physical cores with a single thread per core, ensuring dedicated compute allocation. The instance is configured with 8.0 GB of system memory, providing 4.0 GB per core, and contains no local storage or GPU accelerators. Advanced CPU instruction sets, including AVX, AVX-512, and hardware cryptography, are absent from this profile. This entry-level dedicated instance is designed for workloads requiring predictable CPU performance without resource sharing, making it suitable for lightweight web serving, small databases, and general-purpose development environments.
